intro: Amphitecna sessilifolia, commonly known as "Yerba de la Santa María", is a medicinal plant native to Mexico and Central America, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of A. sessilifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cold, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Mexican medicine, A. sessilifolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
